LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER TO FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR FAILURE TO STATE FACTS SUFFICIENT TO CONSTITUTE A CLAIM FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. DEMURRER TO FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION SUSTAINED FOR NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS AS CONCEDED BY PLAINTIFF. DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON; COURT REPORTER: JOYCE LONG, CSR #8620

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-07-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Kristen Ross, Esq. of Bracamontes & Vlasak, (415) 835-6777 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Rebecca L. Byrne, Esq. of Hassard Bonnington LLP (415) 288-9800 appearing for Defendant and Moving party. Argued for clarification and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer is overruled, 10 days to answer. Order signed in open Court.(302/EHG) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Court Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR# 7489

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-23-2010 Continued To Jun-25-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-16-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer to fifth cause of action sustained with leave to amend for failure to state facts sufficient to constitute a claim for intentional infliction of emotional distress. Demurrer to fourth cause of action sustained for negligent infliction of emotional distress as conceded by Plaintiff. Demurrer overruled as to third cause of action for negligence. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/AJR)Judge: A. James Robertson; Court Reporter: Joyce Long, CSR # 8620
